summary: Iter 61 verified: multi-company compliance batches, employee UAN/ESI/PF pin-login, and payslip auto-email config/dry-run + Resend hook. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_iter61_features.py (11 tests, all pass) and re-ran the iter60 suite for regression — 15/15 still pass. Combined pytest: 26 passed, 0 failed. UI: /pin-login shows all 4 identifier tabs (Mobile/UAN/ESI IP/PF No.), /compliance-salary-run shows Multi-firm mode with ON/OFF toggle at top of ScrollView, and /company-details?company_id=... shows the Payslip auto-email section with a working ON/OFF chip (flip PUTs the config and re-fetch confirms). One minor visual defect found: Payslip auto-email toggle button label renders the literal '\u00b7' escape sequence instead of the middle-dot character '·' because the escape lives in a JSX text child, not a JS string literal.

ui_bugs.issue: Text '\u00b7' is rendered literally instead of the middle-dot character '·' because the unicode escape is placed as JSX text child (JSX children don't interpret \uXXXX escapes). Displays as 'OFF \u00b7 Tap to enable'. Fix: replace the escape with the literal char '·' or wrap in a string expression: {"\u00b7"} — Reference: /app/frontend/app/company-details.tsx:717

action_items: Cosmetic: fix the '\u00b7' literal escape in the Payslip auto-email toggle label at /app/frontend/app/company-details.tsx:717 — replace `\u00b7` with the literal middle-dot '·' or use {"\u00b7"}.
critical_code_review_comments: iter61_features.py: batch max=40 companies enforced and background task correctly marks jobs done/failed with per-firm run_id — verified.
server.py pin_login: UAN validated (10-12 digits), ESI IP / PF No accept any non-empty string. Consider adding a min-length guard for ESI IP / PF too, but not blocking.
generate_payslips_from_run: email hook is best-effort inside a try/except — good, won't fail payslip generation if Resend is down.
PayslipEmailToggleSection wraps API call in try/catch but uses globalThis.alert on web only; native mobile will silently swallow. Consider a shared toast to give feedback on all platforms.
server.py is 11,713 lines — recommend continuing to split into utils/* modules as done for iter60/iter61.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the 3 routes under review; 1 cosmetic text-escape bug

seed_data_creation: Iter61 pin-login test seeds a temp user_id via motor + server._hash_pin with a random UAN/ESI/PF and deletes it in a finally block. No persistent DB pollution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter61 endpoints live at /api/admin/compliance-batches (NOT /compliance-salary-runs/batches — that path is shadowed by the {run_id} wildcard). Company-details page uses ?company_id=... query param, not a /{cid} path segment. Frontend auth token key on web is 'llc_session_token' in localStorage. Emergent LLM key + Resend already configured server-side (RESEND_API_KEY in backend/.env).
rca_of_issue: The '\u00b7' literal appears because JSX child text nodes do NOT interpret \uXXXX unicode escapes (unlike JS/TS string literals). To fix, either paste the actual '·' character in the source, or use a JS expression: {'\u00b7'} which turns it into a real string first. Repro: open /company-details?company_id=<any> as super admin on web — the toggle button reads 'OFF \u00b7 Tap to enable' instead of 'OFF · Tap to enable'.
